summary: Drew DeVault provides his August 2020 status update covering several ongoing projects. The BARE encoding format announced the previous month has gained traction with six language implementations. SourceHut received significant infrastructure improvements including a new server for hg.sr.ht and overhauled PostgreSQL backups. He defers Wayland news to Simon Ser's blog and teases a secret project he's been working on.
key_points: BARE message encoding has gained community adoption with six language implementations after its initial announcement
SourceHut infrastructure was hardened with new servers, improved PostgreSQL backups, and fleet-wide monitoring improvements
Community contributors like наб have been sending patches to SourceHut, showing healthy open-source collaboration
A mysterious secret project is teased but not revealed
money_quotes: The PostgreSQL backup system has been overhauled and made more resilient, both to data loss and to outages.
I'll be working on more user-facing features again next month, but in the meanwhile, contributors like наб have sent many patches.
I definitely haven't been spending a lot of my time on a secret project, no sir.
Or in some cases, the language the author is begrudgingly stuck with.
There are now six projects providing BARE support for their author's favorite programming language.
key_insight: A quiet month focused on essential but unglamorous infrastructure hardening for SourceHut, while the BARE encoding format gains early community traction.
